tone_and_voice: Comprehensive, synthesizing, and donor-facing -- the most complete narrative register in this report family. Annual Impact writing must weave together the full year's evidence (quarterly trends, routine monitoring, the annual round itself) into a single, well-substantiated synthesis, not simply report the annual round's data in isolation.

section_rules.executive_summary: Must synthesize across the full reporting year, not just the annual round's own data -- explicitly note where this year's quarterly and monitoring touchpoints corroborate or complicate the annual finding.
section_rules.discussion: Should explicitly distinguish durable, year-long trends (confirmed by consistency across multiple touchpoints during the year) from single-point annual-round findings -- this distinction is the core analytical value an annual synthesis adds beyond a single survey round.
section_rules.methodology: Note this is the year's largest/most comprehensive sample and describe how it relates to (validates, extends) the year's other monitoring touchpoints.
section_rules.limitations: State clearly which findings are confirmed by multiple touchpoints during the year versus which are annual-round-only observations not yet cross-validated.
section_rules.recommendations: Should explicitly build on the full year's evidence -- e.g. 'given this finding's persistence across Q2, Q3, and this annual round' -- rather than reading as if based on a single round.
section_rules.policy_implications: This is the report type where policy implications carry the most evidentiary weight, given the full-year evidence base -- policy recommendations here should be the most confidently stated in the entire report family.
section_rules.lessons_learned: Reflect on the value of consistent methodology and question wording across quarterly and annual touchpoints, since this consistency is what enables the annual synthesis's strongest claims.
section_rules.conclusion: Must state both the year's central achievement (with full-year evidentiary support) and the year's clearest unresolved item (with its persistence across the year explicitly noted).
section_rules.annexes: Include a full-year data summary showing how quarterly/monitoring touchpoints and the annual round align, since this cross-referencing is the annual report's distinguishing evidentiary feature.
section_rules.evidence_style: Whenever possible, cite corroborating evidence from more than one touchpoint during the year (e.g. 'also observed in Q3 monitoring') to give annual claims their full evidentiary weight -- this is what distinguishes an annual synthesis from a single large survey round.

common_kpis: full-year theme ranking with cross-touchpoint corroboration
persistence/resolution status of items flagged during the year
common_risks: treating the annual round as a standalone data point rather than synthesizing the full year's evidence
double-counting the same underlying issue as if it were multiple independent findings across different reports
overclaiming policy-level conclusions not actually supported by full-year consistency
typical_recommendations: close out any item confirmed persistent across the full year with a specific resolution plan before the next annual cycle
formalize next year's Theory of Change around the year's clearest, most evidenced priority
maintain quarterly-to-annual methodological consistency as a standing practice
typical_policy_implications: the strongest, most evidence-backed policy recommendations in the report family, given the full-year evidence base
sector_knowledge.typical_donor_expectations: Donors reviewing annual impact reports expect the SYNTHESIS value specifically -- a report that simply presents the annual round's numbers without cross-referencing the year's other data sources under-delivers relative to what an annual report should provide.

forbidden_behaviors: Never present the annual round in isolation without attempting to cross-reference it against the year's quarterly/monitoring data where available.
Never claim a finding is a 'durable annual trend' without evidence of consistency across multiple touchpoints during the year.
Never carry an unresolved item into the next annual report without explicitly noting its multi-cycle persistence and the accountability implication of that persistence.
